data.title: Each of the U.S. special operations aircraft destroyed in Iran was worth over $100 million.
data.description: According to a report by the Wall Street Journal, the U.S. Air Force Special Operations Command stated that each MC-130J aircraft used in the Iranian operation to rescue the crew of a downed U.S. F-15E fighter jet is worth over $100 million. This type of aircraft is designed to deploy troops to hostile areas and conduct withdrawal operations, possesses aerial refueling capabilities, and is equipped with advanced sensors for defense against air defense interception, including a heat-tracking system. A U.S. official stated that the U.S. military destroyed two MC-130J aircraft during the rescue operation. The U.S. official did not specify the cause of the problems encountered by the aircraft. A senior Iranian official released photos of the burned wreckage of at least one aircraft. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted on social media: "If the United States achieves three more such 'victories,' it will be utterly destroyed."

data.title: Market Sentiment Suggests Potential Improvement Amid High FUD Levels
data.description: Santiment has indicated that market trends often move contrary to public expectations. According to ChainCatcher, the current high levels of fear, uncertainty, and doubt (FUD) are seen as a positive signal, suggesting that market conditions may improve in the near future.

data.title: An address deposited 1856 ETH into OKX; selling them would result in a loss of $1.89 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an address deposited 1,856 ETH into OKX 45 minutes ago, worth approximately $3.78 million. This address held ETH for two months, of which 1,450 ETH were withdrawn from the exchange on January 18th at a price of $3,339.38. Selling them would have resulted in a loss of $1.89 million, representing a 39% decrease in assets.

data.title: Santiment: Bearish sentiment on Bitcoin reaches a 5-week high
data.description: According to Santiment data, bearish sentiment towards Bitcoin has reached a 5-week high.

data.title: Solana co-founder Toly: Cheaper, faster, and lower-risk blockchain finance will ultimately prevail.
data.description: In response to a tweet from Helius CEO stating that "the next crypto cycle is coming," Solana co-founder Toly replied that cheaper, faster, and less risky financial methods will ultimately outperform all alternatives. This is based on immutable, formally verified software. Administrator keys contain on-chain, immutable policies, such as time locks. All the truly interesting things in finance, such as criminals, involve an extremely wide range of aspects. Therefore, anything complex requires administrator privileges.

data.title: Paul Chan: Hong Kong is a safe haven for investors' funds
data.description: Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan Mo-po stated in a blog post on March 5th that the average daily turnover of Hong Kong stocks exceeded HK$300 billion in March, an increase of over 8% compared to the same period last year. This reflects Hong Kong's status as a reliable safe haven for capital, coupled with the stable economic growth of mainland China and the large number of high-quality companies listing in Hong Kong, providing investors with abundant investment opportunities. Chan noted that as of March 27th, Hong Kong's IPO fundraising this year had exceeded HK$103 billion, ranking first globally. Including subsequent financing, the total fundraising amount is approximately HK$237 billion. More importantly, an increasing number of companies listing in Hong Kong are emerging industries, with over 500 applications currently in the queue. It can be said that more and more companies view Hong Kong as an important window for financing and overseas expansion. (China News Network)

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Positions Reach $3.532 Billion
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass reveals that the Hyperliquid platform currently holds whale positions totaling $3.532 billion. The platform's long positions amount to $1.819 billion, representing 51.51% of the total, while short positions stand at $1.712 billion, accounting for 48.49%. The long positions have incurred a loss of $127 million, whereas the short positions have gained $129 million.

Notably, a significant whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has engaged in a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, resulting in an unrealized loss of $7.6865 million.

data.title: Dragonfly transfers $2.12 million MNT to Bybit
data.description: According to Arkham, Dragonfly transferred $2.12 million worth of MNT to Bybit. This is its first withdrawal from a known crypto wallet in two months and its largest holding outside of BGB. Dragonfly currently holds only $107.29 million worth of BGB and $1.92 million worth of other cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Monad's Layer 1 Blockchain Achieves $400 Million in DeFi Total Value Locked
data.description: Monad has announced that the total value locked (TVL) in DeFi on its Layer 1 blockchain has reached $400 million. According to Foresight News, this milestone highlights the growing interest and investment in decentralized finance platforms. The achievement underscores the potential of Layer 1 blockchains in supporting DeFi applications and attracting significant capital inflows.

data.title: Product Manager X: Approximately 80% of cryptocurrency post activity is driven by bots.
data.description: Coin Bureau published an article on the X platform stating that Nikita Bier, the product manager of X, said that there is no technology that can solve the problem of spam under cryptocurrency posts because about 80% of the activity is driven by bots.

data.title: Drift Protocol Wallet Address Clarification
data.description: A wallet address previously reported has been confirmed by Drift Protocol to belong to an investor rather than the team. According to Foresight News, Onchain Lens provided clarification on the matter, correcting earlier reports about the ownership of the wallet address.

data.title: US media: The rescued American pilot has been taken to Kuwait.
data.description: According to a report by The New York Times on the 5th, the second pilot from the downed US F-15E fighter jet has been transferred to Kuwait for medical treatment. During the rescue operation, the US military destroyed two "stranded" US transport planes involved in the rescue mission. The report stated that the pilot had been evading Iranian searches in mountainous terrain for over 24 hours, carrying only a pistol. The US military deployed hundreds of special operations personnel to complete the rescue, during which a firefight occurred with Iranian forces. (Xinhua)

data.title: Solana Co-Founder Discusses Future of Financial Solutions
data.description: Solana co-founder Toly responded to a tweet by Helius CEO about the upcoming crypto cycle, emphasizing that cheaper, faster, and less risky financial methods will ultimately surpass all alternatives. According to Foresight News, Toly highlighted the importance of immutable, formally verified software, noting that administrator keys include on-chain immutable strategies such as time locks. He remarked that all genuinely interesting aspects of finance, including criminal activities, involve a wide range of elements, indicating that complex matters are inseparable from administrative privileges.

data.title: Iranian Forces Reportedly Destroy U.S. Aircraft Amid Rescue Mission
data.description:
On April 5, Iranian military forces claimed to have destroyed a U.S. aircraft during a rescue mission for a downed pilot. According to BlockBeats, the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters of Iran's armed forces announced that the Revolutionary Guard and Basij militia, with the support of law enforcement commandos, targeted and destroyed the enemy aircraft south of Isfahan.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps also stated that multiple Iranian military departments collaborated to destroy the aircraft involved in the U.S. pilot rescue mission. However, this claim has not been confirmed by U.S. sources.

On the same day, The New York Times reported that two U.S. military transport planes encountered malfunctions during the rescue operation in Iran, leaving them stranded. The U.S. military subsequently dispatched three additional aircraft to continue the mission and destroyed the two malfunctioning transport planes.

data.title: Iran has allowed Iraqi ships to use the Strait of Hormuz, resulting in a record weekly traffic volume since the war began.
data.description: Over the past two weeks, the number of ships transiting the "blocked" Strait of Hormuz has been steadily increasing. More and more ships from friendly nations are passing through the strait. According to Bloomberg, as traffic in the Strait of Hormuz gradually recovered over the past week, the seven-day rolling average of traffic as of Friday reached its highest level since the outbreak of the war. An increasing number of ships are crossing the strait, including some with no clear connection to Iran. Countries are negotiating with Tehran to allow their ships passage. Liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) carriers dominated the transit over the past day, including one bound for India and others with ties to Iran. According to Bloomberg's calculations, a total of 13 ships have transited since Friday morning. Of these, 10 sailed out of the Persian Gulf and 3 entered from international waters, according to ship tracking data compiled by Bloomberg.

data.title: Traffic Through Hormuz Strait Reaches Peak Levels Since Conflict
data.description:
The number of vessels passing through the Hormuz Strait has significantly increased over the past two weeks. According to Odaily, friendly nations' ships are increasingly navigating the strait. Bloomberg reports that traffic through the Hormuz Strait has gradually risen over the past week, reaching its highest rolling average in seven days since the outbreak of conflict.

A growing number of ships are traversing the strait, including some not directly linked to Iran. Countries are negotiating with Tehran to secure passage for their vessels. In the past day, liquefied petroleum gas carriers have dominated the traffic, including a ship bound for India and others associated with Iran.

Bloomberg's calculations indicate that since Friday morning, 13 vessels have crossed the strait. Of these, 10 exited the Persian Gulf, while 3 entered from international waters.

data.title: Iranian media: Shot down a US C-130 military transport plane and two Black Hawk helicopters
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, a spokesperson for the Joint Command of the Iranian Armed Forces stated that the downed US aircraft consisted of a C-130 military transport plane and two Black Hawk helicopters. (Jinshi)

data.title: BNB Drops Below 590 USDT with a 0.13% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 05, 2026, 06:10 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 590 USDT and is now trading at 589.219971 USDT, with a narrowed 0.13% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Yemen's Houthi Forces Claim Joint Strike on Israel with Iran and Lebanon
data.description: On April 4, local time, Yemen's Houthi forces announced a joint operation with Iran and Lebanon targeting sites within Israel. According to Jin10, the Houthis stated that they launched a ballistic missile equipped with a cluster warhead and multiple drones to strike Ben Gurion Airport near Tel Aviv and other significant military and strategic targets in southern Israel. The group claimed that the operation achieved its intended objectives.

data.title: Bitcoin social media bearish sentiment hits 5-week high
data.description: Santiment reports that bearish comments on Bitcoin on social media have climbed to a 5-week high. Data shows a bullish-to-bearish ratio of 0.81, meaning approximately 5 bearish comments for every 4 bullish comments. Santiment notes that markets typically move in the opposite direction of popular expectations, and such high levels of FUD (Fear, Uncertainty, and Greed) sentiment are often a common element in price rallies, potentially indicating a faster-than-expected price recovery. Furthermore, the cryptocurrency FUD index scored 12 on Sunday, remaining in the extreme fear zone. Bitcoin is currently priced at $67,100, down 5.53% over the past 30 days.
